steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Adlhart claims the sponge would be difficult to mass-produce. We need a finding that directly supports a production barrier — something that prevents scaling up manufacturing.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Textiles are one of the largest sources of microplastic pollution in our oceans.: describes the pollution problem, not anything about producing the sponge. ✗
  • Choice B — The sponge created by Lin and his team has a relatively light weight for its volume, making it potentially easy to ship to different locations.: addresses shipping logistics, not production constraints. ✗
  • Choice C — The absorption capabilities of the sponge created by Lin and his team varied depending on the liquid solution used in the team's experiments.: describes performance variability, not a barrier to mass production. ✗
  • Choice D — The worldwide supply of starch and gelatin is in high demand from the food industry, so those substances would be difficult to source in significant quantities.: directly establishes a raw-material sourcing barrier — the sponge's main ingredients are hard to obtain at scale. ✓
That is the correct answer.
